author: Dr. David Park
content:
Telephone encounter with patient Robert Morrison regarding pulmonology follow-up and prior authorization.

REASON FOR CALL: Patient's pharmacy (CVS) contacted office regarding prior authorization needed for home CPAP supplies. Patient also called separately with questions about his sleep apnea management.

PRIOR AUTHORIZATION:
- CPAP supplies (mask, tubing, filters) — PA submitted to Blue Cross Blue Shield. Patient's compliance data downloaded from machine: average usage 5.2 hours/night over last 90 days, AHI on therapy 3.4 events/hour (well controlled). Meets insurance compliance threshold of ≥4 hours on ≥70% of nights. PA should be approved within 5-7 business days.

SYMPTOM REVIEW:
- Patient reports he is tolerating CPAP well. Using full-face mask (ResMed AirFit F20). Wife confirms he is using it nightly.
- Daytime sleepiness: improved since CPAP initiation. Epworth Sleepiness Scale informally assessed — patient estimates 6/24 (mild, improved from 14 at diagnosis).
- No complaints of mask leak, aerophagia, or nasal congestion.
- Patient asked about his heart failure and sleep apnea connection — explained that treating OSA helps reduce cardiac strain and may help with his HFrEF management. Encouraged continued compliance.

CHART REVIEW: Noted patient has multiple comorbidities including HFrEF (EF 35%), A-fib, T2DM, CKD 3b. OSA is an important modifiable contributor to cardiac risk. His good CPAP compliance is beneficial.

ADDITIONAL: Patient mentioned occasional morning headaches — reviewed that this can occur with mask leak or inadequate pressure. Current pressure setting 10 cmH2O (auto-titrated range 8-12). Data shows minimal leak. Headaches may also be related to his blood pressure — advised checking morning BPs and reporting to PCP if consistently elevated.

PLAN:
1. PA submitted for CPAP supplies — will notify patient when approved.
2. Continue current CPAP settings (auto 8-12 cmH2O).
3. Annual sleep medicine follow-up due — scheduling for May 2022.
4. Morning headaches — monitor BPs, report to PCP Dr. Chen if persistent.
5. Encouraged continued excellent CPAP compliance.

Total call duration: 15 minutes.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented.

Electronically signed by: Dr. David Park
Date: 2/15/2022
